title: Route Calls to an Agent
lede: Decide exactly what happens the moment someone dials your number — send callers to an AI agent 24/7, switch agents by time of day, forward to a human line with an AI safety net, or hand the call off entirely. It's all configured per number, in one panel.

steps.title: Open the phone number you want to configure
steps.content:
Every routing decision lives on the number itself, so start at your numbers list.

  1. In the left sidebar, click Phone numbers (under Call Center).
  2. Make sure you're on the Numbers tab. The list shows each number with its Label, Capabilities (Voice, SMS, provider, WhatsApp), and an Inbound Agent column — this column tells you at a glance what each number routes to. It reads None until you assign an agent.
  3. Click the phone number (the blue link in the Phone Number column) or the green eye icon under Actions to open its details panel.
💡 If the Inbound Agent column shows Unknown in orange, the assigned agent was deleted or isn't visible to your account — open the number and pick a new one.

images.alt: Phone numbers list with the Inbound Agent column
images.caption: The Inbound Agent column shows what each number currently routes to.

images.alt: Phone numbers list with the Inbound Agent column
images.caption: The Inbound Agent column shows what each number currently routes to.

steps.title: Pick a Call Handling mode
steps.content:
The Phone Number Details panel opens on the Settings tab, in the Inbound Settings card. The Call Handling switch at the top is the master decision — it has three modes:

  • Enable Agents — an AI agent answers every incoming call (and you can rotate agents on a schedule). This is the default and the most popular mode.
  • Warm Transfer — calls are forwarded to a real phone number (your office line, a cell phone) while iMash stays on the line, so an AI agent can step in if nobody picks up.
  • Cold Transfer — calls are immediately handed off to another number and leave the platform entirely.

The panel also has Inbound Calls and Outbound Calls tabs (this number's own call history) plus two buttons at the bottom: Test Call and Save Settings.

images.alt: Phone Number Details panel showing the Call Handling modes
images.caption: Inbound Settings — choose Enable Agents, Warm Transfer, or Cold Transfer.

images.alt: Phone Number Details panel showing the Call Handling modes
images.caption: Inbound Settings — choose Enable Agents, Warm Transfer, or Cold Transfer.

steps.title: Assign the AI agent that answers calls
steps.content:
This is the magic moment — one dropdown and your number answers itself, day and night.

  1. With Call Handling set to Enable Agents, click the Inbound Agent dropdown.
  2. Pick the agent that should answer this number. Only agents you've already created appear here — if the list is empty, build one first (see Create an AI Agent below).
  3. Click Save Settings in the bottom-right. The button lights up blue whenever you have unsaved changes.

From this moment, every call to this number is answered by that agent. You can clear the assignment anytime with the small × inside the dropdown.

⚠️ Nothing is applied until you click Save Settings — if you close the panel with the button still blue, your changes are lost.

images.alt: Inbound Agent dropdown open with a list of AI agents
images.caption: Pick any of your AI agents from the Inbound Agent dropdown.

images.alt: Inbound Agent dropdown open with a list of AI agents
images.caption: Pick any of your AI agents from the Inbound Agent dropdown.

steps.title: Optional: rotate agents by time of day (after-hours routing)
steps.content:
Want your sales agent during business hours and an after-hours message-taker at night? The Assistant Scheduler does exactly that.

  1. Still in Enable Agents mode, tick Enable Scheduler on the Assistant Scheduler card. (The plain Inbound Agent dropdown greys out and shows Managed by Scheduler — the schedule is now in charge.)
  2. Set the Timezone — all time slots are evaluated in this timezone, so pick where your business operates.
  3. Pick a Default Assistant. This agent answers whenever no time slot matches — it is effectively your after-hours agent.
  4. Click Add Time Slot. For each slot, tick the Days it applies to (Sun–Sat), set the Start and End times, and choose the Agent that answers during that window.
  5. Add as many slots as you need (weekday daytime, weekend mornings, etc.), then click Save Settings.
⚠️ Slots must not overlap on the same day — if they do, a red Schedule Overlap Detected alert appears and Save Settings stays disabled until you fix the times.

images.alt: Assistant Scheduler with timezone, default assistant, and a weekday time slot
images.caption: One slot for business hours; the Default Assistant covers everything else.

images.alt: Assistant Scheduler with timezone, default assistant, and a weekday time slot
images.caption: One slot for business hours; the Default Assistant covers everything else.

steps.title: Optional: forward to a real phone with Warm Transfer
steps.content:
Prefer that a human picks up first? Warm Transfer forwards incoming calls to any phone number while iMash stays on the line — so the call is still tracked and recorded, and your AI can catch whatever the human misses.

  1. Set Call Handling to Warm Transfer.
  2. In Forward To Number, enter the destination phone number. Use the small + / No + switch on the right to control whether the number is dialed in international format (with +) or as a plain digit string — most numbers should keep +.
  3. Optionally pick a Forward Agent — the AI agent associated with the warm transfer.
  4. Click Save Settings.

images.alt: Warm Transfer settings with Forward To Number and Forward Agent
images.caption: Warm Transfer keeps iMash on the line while ringing your real phone.

images.alt: Warm Transfer settings with Forward To Number and Forward Agent
images.caption: Warm Transfer keeps iMash on the line while ringing your real phone.

steps.title: Give Warm Transfer an AI safety net
steps.content:
The Advanced Call Routing card (visible only in Warm Transfer mode) is where you make sure no call is ever truly missed. Each option is a Disabled / Enabled switch that reveals an agent picker when enabled:

  • Send Busy Calls to AI — if the forwarded line is busy, the selected AI agent takes the call instead of the caller hearing a busy tone.
  • Auto Dial Missed Calls — if a call is missed, the selected AI agent automatically calls the customer back.
  • No Answer Timeout — if nobody answers within the number of seconds you set (default 30), the selected AI agent picks up the call.
  1. Flip the options you want to Enabled.
  2. Pick an agent for each enabled option (they can all be the same agent).
  3. For No Answer Timeout, set how many seconds the phone should ring first.
  4. Click Save Settings.
💡 The combination "Warm Transfer + No Answer Timeout" is the classic small-business setup: your phone rings for 25–30 seconds, and if you can't grab it, the AI answers so the lead never goes to voicemail — or to a competitor.

images.alt: Advanced Call Routing with busy, missed-call, and no-answer AI fallbacks enabled
images.caption: Each fallback reveals its own agent picker when enabled.

images.alt: Advanced Call Routing with busy, missed-call, and no-answer AI fallbacks enabled
images.caption: Each fallback reveals its own agent picker when enabled.

steps.title: Optional: hand calls off completely with Cold Transfer
steps.content:
Cold Transfer immediately forwards every incoming call to another number using a SIP REFER — a blind handoff with no warm introduction and no AI involvement.

  1. Set Call Handling to Cold Transfer.
  2. Enter the destination in Forward To Number (same + / No + switch as Warm Transfer).
  3. Click Save Settings.
⚠️ As the blue info card in the panel explains: once a cold transfer completes, iMash can no longer track or record the call — you lose visibility of everything after the handoff. If you want recordings, analytics, or AI fallbacks, use Warm Transfer instead.

images.alt: Cold Transfer settings with the SIP REFER information card
images.caption: Cold Transfer hands the call off entirely — no tracking after the handoff.

images.alt: Cold Transfer settings with the SIP REFER information card
images.caption: Cold Transfer hands the call off entirely — no tracking after the handoff.

steps.title: Routing to a human team instead of an AI
steps.content:
Assigning an inbound agent covers AI-first answering, but you can also ring a whole team of live operators:

  • Ring a human group directly — a number can route to a Contact Center permission group so every online member's dialer rings at once and the first to answer takes the call. This is set up on the group, not on the number — see the Inbound SIP Routing guide.
  • AI first, humans on demand — keep the AI agent as the front door and give it a transfer tool, so it hands callers to your team only when needed (see Call Transfer).
  • Humans first, AI backup — use Warm Transfer to your team's line with the No Answer Timeout fallback from the previous step.

Mix and match per number — your main line can be AI-first while a VIP line rings straight to your senior team.

steps.title: Assign one agent to many numbers at once
steps.content:
Managing dozens of numbers? Don't open them one by one.

  1. On the Numbers list, tick the checkbox at the left of each number you want to change (the header checkbox selects the whole page). A N selected counter and a bulk toolbar appear.
  2. Click the Inbound Agent button and pick an agent from the dropdown — it's applied to every selected number instantly.
  3. To unassign in bulk, choose Clear Inbound Agent at the bottom of the same dropdown.

The same toolbar also offers Edit Label (rename many numbers at once) and Delete Selected.

images.alt: Two numbers selected with the bulk Inbound Agent dropdown open
images.caption: Select several numbers, then set or clear their inbound agent in one click.

images.alt: Two numbers selected with the bulk Inbound Agent dropdown open
images.caption: Select several numbers, then set or clear their inbound agent in one click.

steps.title: Test your routing and confirm it works
steps.content:
Time for the payoff — hear your number answer itself.

  1. In the number's details panel, click Test Call (bottom-left of the Settings tab). This opens the built-in web dialer pre-loaded with this number and its inbound agent so you can talk to your setup without picking up a phone.
  2. Then do a real-world check: dial the number from your cell phone — your assigned agent (or forward destination) should pick up exactly as configured.
  3. Open the Inbound Calls tab in the same panel — every incoming call to this number appears here with its date, cost, ended reason, and duration. The Outbound Calls tab shows calls placed from this number.
  4. For the full cross-number picture, go to Call Center → Call Logs and open your test call to review the recording and transcript.

images.alt: Inbound Calls tab inside the Phone Number Details panel
images.caption: Each number keeps its own inbound and outbound call history.

images.alt: Inbound Calls tab inside the Phone Number Details panel
images.caption: Each number keeps its own inbound and outbound call history.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials
